context.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Apply one legal operation and observe stable post-operation behavior.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['din_serial', 'din_valid']
Drive a short legal sequence of din_serial bits with din_valid asserted to verify basic accumulation behavior.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe after each clock edge; no fixed latency assumed.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Exercise boundary-value and width-sensitive input patterns.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['din_valid']
Toggle din_valid between 0 and 1 to explore boundary behavior of the valid handshake.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Conservative observation after each toggle.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Observe repeated or back-to-back legal operations under conservative timing assumptions.
stimulus_intent: Apply two legal operations with minimal idle spacing that remains safe for the current contract strength.
Apply two back‑to‑back 8‑bit serial transfers with minimal idle cycles to verify that the module correctly resets its internal counter and produces distinct valid outputs.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Conservative observation after each rising edge; no assumption of fixed latency between sequences.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Verify that illegal input patterns (din_valid asserted without proper sequencing) do not cause spurious dout_valid.
stimulus_intent: Assert din_valid=1 while din_serial is unchanged to test handling of unexpected valid assertion.
timing_assumptions: Observe after at least one rising edge of clk following stimulus.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Confirm that dout_valid aligns with the accumulation of eight serial bits.
stimulus_intent: Drive a sequence of eight din_serial bits with din_valid=1 each cycle, then deassert din_valid and observe dout_valid and dout_parallel.
timing_assumptions: Conservative observation after each rising edge of clk.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

generated_files.description: Marks the rendered cocotb test directory as a Python package.
Conservative DUT interface helpers derived from the contract artifact.
Thin environment wrapper that composes interface, timing-safe waits, and coverage helpers.
Structured oracle helper functions rendered from the oracle artifact.
Stable runtime helpers exposed to LLM-filled stimulus and oracle TODO blocks.
Coverage scaffold rendered from plan coverage tags for later execution stages.
Rendered basic cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Rendered protocol cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Rendered edge cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Phase 4 executable Makefile shell; Phase 5 injects run-time variables and executes it.
